patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/454

## Problem
(1) `pm schema add-field` accepts reserved built-in field names (e.g. customer_impact — an existing create flag) with no warning; (2) when an extension later registers the same name, the runtime error "collides with reserved item metadata" names neither the reserved-ness nor the collision partner; in the GH-454 dogfood the config-field + extension-field combination broke ALL pm create Task calls.

## Proposed fix
add-field validates against the reserved/built-in name list at registration time (error, or --force with warning); collision errors name both claimants (config field vs extension field vs reserved metadata) with a rename hint. Share the same RESERVED_ITEM_FIELD_NAMES source pm-ghf1 exports to the SDK — this is the CLI/config-side twin of that SDK-side item.

## Definition of done
add-field on a reserved name fails fast with the reserved list; collision error names both parties; shared constant with runtime + SDK (drift spec); repro from GH-454 covered by spec.
GH-454: schema add-field accepts reserved built-in names silently; collision error names no partner

Dogfood GH-454: registering a reserved built-in name (customer_impact) via add-field succeeds silently, then extension collisions produce a partner-less misleading error; in the dogfood the combination broke all Task creates. CLI/config-side twin of pm-ghf1 (SDK lint/preflight side). Duplicate check: pm-ghf1 covers only the SDK surface; no other overlap.
add-field fails fast on reserved/built-in names with the reserved list; runtime collision errors name both claimants with rename hint; shared constant with runtime+SDK pinned by drift spec

author: codex-root
message: Start broad SDK workspace-customization implementation: promote schema/config/profile/init primitives and fix the linked schema/init correctness regressions in one shared verification lane.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation start: selected after live all-status duplicate checks, full metadata/comments/notes/learnings review, GitHub issue/PR/security/dependency inspection, and SDK-first frontier reconciliation. This item is actively edited in the broad schema/config/profile/init SDK promotion bundle; unrelated open items remain unclaimed.

value.note: Expand shared runtime-schema reserved key contract

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence: expanded the schema reserved-key contract to cover built-in mutation metadata including customer_impact/expected_result; collision errors now identify custom/extension and built-in claimants, include a rename hint, and enumerate the shared denylist. Focused schema/extension tests, typecheck, lint/static gates, and packed-tarball rejection proof passed.

patch.value: Reserved schema/extension collisions now fail at registration/use with both claimants, rename guidance, and the full denylist; regression and packed-package proof passed.
Fixed in PR#520: reserved-field list derived from canonical front-matter metadata; add-field and runtime collisions fail fast naming both claimants with rename hint.
Reserved/built-in schema field names rejected at registration and use, with the full reserved list and both claimants named.
Collisions failed silently or named no partner; ops-profile keys like customer_impact/expected_result were missing from the denylist.
